item.title: Is Israel really isolated? Polls and diplomacy point in opposite directions
item.description: Trump says the Saudi Arabia nuclear deal hinges on joining the Abraham Accords, as experts debate whether Israel faces isolation or expanding ties.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump predicted in recent days that additional countries would soon join the Abraham Accords, renewing debate over whether Israel is becoming increasingly isolated or continuing to expand its most important strategic relationships.

"The Abraham Accords have been, I think, a tremendous success," Trump said during a Tuesday Oval Office meeting with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun. "I think you’re going to see a lot of countries joining pretty soon."

Trump's prediction underscores a central debate about Israel's global position: Public support for the country has fallen sharply in the United States and across much of the world, yet Israel continues to build or preserve diplomatic, military, energy and technology relationships with foreign governments.

TRUMP CALLS ON ARAB NATIONS TO SIGN ABRAHAM ACCORDS

The Abraham Accords, brokered by the Trump administration in 2020, normalized relations between Israel and the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain, Morocco and Sudan. Trump has made expanding the agreements a central goal of his Middle East strategy. 

The dispute depends partly on how isolation is measured. Critics point to worsening public opinion, pressure at international institutions and growing opposition among Democrats and younger voters. Israeli officials and conservative foreign-policy experts argue that government-to-government cooperation remains strong and, in some regions, is expanding.

Trump reinforced that strategy Thursday by saying a U.S. civilian nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia would depend on the kingdom joining the Abraham Accords. The statement showed the administration attempting to use one of Washington’s most valuable offers to Riyadh to secure normalization with Israel, but it did not demonstrate that Saudi Arabia had agreed to do so.

Israeli Ambassador to the United Nations Danny Danon rejected the contention that Israel had become isolated.

"The claim that Israel is an isolated country is simply not true," Danon told Fox News Digital. "While the campaign to dismantle the Hamas terrorist organization has drawn international criticism, Israel’s relationships with other countries have not weakened. They have evolved."

Former White House national security official Richard Goldberg similarly argued that Israel retains strong support among conservatives and that its electoral gains are translating into closer government relations.

"Polling in the United States shows massive support among conservatives and Republicans, including self-described MAGA, for Israel still," Goldberg told Fox News Digital. "And that’s reflected around the world, particularly among conservative candidates and parties that win elected office right now."

Goldberg pointed to political changes in Latin America, expanding partnerships in the Eastern Mediterranean and Israel’s continuing defense and technology relationships as evidence that public criticism has not translated into comprehensive strategic isolation.

The dispute comes amid polling that shows Israel retaining deep support among Republicans while losing ground across the broader American electorate and much of the world.

POTENTIAL 2028 DEM CONTENDER UNLOADS ON NETANYAHU, ADMITS US-ISRAEL TIES AT 'CROSSROADS'

Eighty percent of self-identified MAGA Republicans said Israel’s security matters to U.S. security and prosperity, a Reagan Institute survey released in June found, while 73% supported sending Israel U.S. weapons. Among MAGA Republicans under 30, 63% said Israel’s security matters to the United States and 60% supported sending weapons.

The same survey found that broader support was narrowing. Sixty-five percent of Americans said Israel’s security matters to U.S. security and prosperity, down six percentage points from a year earlier. 

The figure fell nine points among Democrats to 57%, compared with 75% of Republicans and 80% of MAGA Republicans.

A Pew Research Center survey published in June found that a median of 67% of adults across 36 countries viewed Israel unfavorably, while 25% viewed it favorably. The findings came from Pew’s Spring 2026 Global Attitudes Survey.

In the United States, 60% of adults viewed Israel unfavorably, up from 53% in 2025 and 42% in 2022, according to a separate Pew survey conducted March 23 to March 29. Eighty percent of Democrats and Democratic-leaning independents held an unfavorable view, while negative assessments were also widespread among adults under 50.

Israel no longer held a statistically significant advantage over the Palestinians in Americans’ sympathies, Gallup reported in February. Forty-one percent said they sympathized more with the Palestinians and 36% with the Israelis, although Gallup said the five-point difference was not statistically significant. 

In 2023, 54% sympathized more with Israelis and 31% with Palestinians.

Former Chicago Mayor Rahm Emanuel highlighted that erosion during a July 8 speech at Tel Aviv University.

"Support for Israel around the world is declining," Emanuel said, "The only diplomatic achievement Israel has secured in the past three years is Somaliland."

THE US-ISRAEL STRATEGIC ALLIANCE – PARTNERSHIP, NOT CHARITY

Emanuel’s conclusion focuses on Israel’s declining public legitimacy and its strained position in traditional diplomatic institutions. Goldberg and Danon argue that it overlooks expanding government-to-government relationships built around defense, intelligence, energy, technology and trade.

Goldberg pointed in particular to Latin America, where he said newly elected conservative governments have made closer ties with Israel an early priority.

"Every time a government in South America right now flips from left wing to right wing, one of the first things they do is reestablish better relations with Israel, withdraw recognition of a Palestinian state and see what else they can do to make Israel a strategic partner," Goldberg said.

He cited Argentina under President Javier Milei and the proposed "Isaac Accords," along with political changes in Chile, Colombia and Bolivia, as evidence of a broader realignment.

Goldberg also argued that Israel remains central to emerging partnerships involving energy, defense, intelligence and critical infrastructure.

"If you’re in the Eastern Mediterranean and you’re Greece and you are Cyprus, you’re thinking about how do we work with Israel to make the Eastern Mediterranean partnership even stronger," he said. "If you’re Armenia and Azerbaijan in a tough neighborhood between Turkey and Iran, you’re talking to Israel, you’re getting closer to Israel."

Israel, Greece and Cyprus reaffirmed their strategic partnership during a trilateral summit in December 2025, agreeing to expand cooperation in energy, security, emergency preparedness and regional connectivity.

Danon said the diplomatic shift is visible even inside the United Nations, an institution where Israel routinely faces critical resolutions and public condemnation.

"We see this every day in the corridors of the United Nations," he said. "While many South American countries were once highly critical of Israel in recent years, we are now cooperating with many of them. These discussions are happening in public and behind the scenes."

"As Israel’s official representative to the United Nations, I regularly meet with ambassadors from around the world, including from Arab countries, to quietly advance initiatives where we share common interests," Danon said.

Danon went further than Goldberg in assessing Israel’s position since the Oct. 7, 2023, Hamas terrorist attacks and the subsequent war.

"Compared to the period before October 7, Israel is in a far stronger geostrategic position," Danon said. "That is reflected not only in regional developments but also in the country’s diplomatic relationships around the world."

The competing evidence suggests that much of the disagreement depends on how isolation is defined.

Israel may be losing public support and influence in traditional international institutions while simultaneously preserving or expanding government-to-government cooperation in defense, technology, intelligence, energy and agriculture.

MAMDANI GOES BACK ON CAMPAIGN PROMISE, SAYS NYC CAN’T ARREST NETANYAHU 

Goldberg acknowledged that relationships driven by conservative political victories could become vulnerable when governments change.

But he argued that Israel is increasingly building relationships around durable national interests rather than political ideology alone.

The central risk for Israel is that today’s collapse in public opinion could eventually reshape tomorrow’s government policies. Conservative governments may currently protect and expand strategic ties, but younger voters and left-wing parties could alter those relationships after future elections.

Israel is scheduled to hold national elections Oct. 27, the country’s first since the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks.

Goldberg argued that a change in Israel’s government would provide a test of whether international opposition is directed primarily at Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu or at Israel itself.

"If there is somebody else who becomes prime minister next, we will have to evaluate," Goldberg said. "Those very loud detractors of Israel who right now use Netanyahu as their symbol for bashing and say it’s not about Israel, it’s about Netanyahu, they’ll be put to the test very quickly."

He noted that the boycott movement and anti-Israel campus organizations remained active during the previous government led by Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid, when Netanyahu was not prime minister.

"Is their hatred and venom really just about Bibi Netanyahu, or is it fundamentally about a Jewish state that they don’t like existing?" Goldberg asked.

For now, both realities appear to be unfolding at once: Israel remains a sought-after military, intelligence and technology partner for a growing group of governments, while its support among the global public has fallen to levels that could eventually threaten those relationships.

item.title: WATCH: Cory Mills blasts DOJ probe as ‘political op' ahead of Florida primary
item.description: Cory Mills claims the reported DOJ investigation is a political attack by his opponents weeks before Florida's Republican primary on Aug. 18.
item.content:encoded:
Rep. Cory Mills, R-Fla., is forcefully denying reports that he is under criminal investigation by the Justice Department, claiming the allegations are part of a politically-motivated effort to damage his re-election campaign weeks before Florida's Republican primary.

"If you also read the rest of the reports, it never says that the DOJ or the FBI has ever said there's an investigation," Mills told Fox News Digital. "All it ever says is that a source claims the following."

A source familiar told Fox News that the DOJ is investigating Mills but did not specify exactly what he is being investigated for. ABC News later reported, citing additional sources, that the investigation is tied at least in part to Mills' finances.

DEM SENATOR FACES DOJ PROBE AFTER ALLEGATIONS OF SPENDING CAMPAIGN FUNDS ON LUXURY LIFESTYLE

But Mills denies that neither the DOJ nor the FBI have ever reached out to him about any probe.

"I have no indication," Mills said about being contacted about an investigation. "No one's ever reached out to us. And the DOJ, FBI themselves have never even confirmed such an investigation exists."

The Florida Republican claimed that the timing of the unsubstantiated allegation indicates that the claim comes from one of his opponents in the upcoming race to fill a House seat representing Florida’s 7th Congressional District.

"This is clearly a political op by our opponents, and they're hoping that within four weeks left to go that they can use this to try and slander my name a little bit further," Mills said.

"It's ‘source on the inside with knowledge,’" Mills said. "Well, if it's a source on the inside with knowledge, then why can't they give the details of what exactly they're supposed to be investigating?"

NEW EXPLOSIVE ALLEGATION ROCKS PLATNER CAMPAIGN STILL REELING FROM RAPE ACCUSATION

There are three candidates running against Mills in Florida’s upcoming Republican primary — former newscaster Ryan Elijah, former Defense Department official Michael Johnson and former business executive Sarah Ulrich. Mills’ main competitor in the race is Elijah.

The DOJ probe claims come as the House Ethics Committee currently has a separate investigation open on Mills. He is being investigated for allegations of sexual misconduct and dating violence, along with violations of his campaign finances.

Mills was accused of domestic abuse in an incident in Feb. 2025. He was also accused of blackmail later that same year, alleging he threatened to release a romantic partner’s explicit pictures.

Rep. Nancy Mace, R-S.C., filed a motion to expel Mills in April, also on claims of sexual misconduct and campaign finance violations but additionally including misrepresenting his military service and unlawful involvement in federal contracts while serving in Congress.

RESIGNATION IS THE NEW ESCAPE HATCH AS LAWMAKERS FACE EXPULSION

Mills has denied any allegations of wrongdoings in all three efforts against him.

The House Ethics investigation began in Nov 2025, and it has not been specified when it will end.

Mills was first elected to serve in the House in 2022 and was re-elected in 2024, now serving on the House Armed Services and Intelligence committees. He is seeking a third House term.

Florida’s primary race is set to take place Aug. 18.

item.title: Trump heads back to correspondents’ dinner months after gunfire forced evacuation
item.description: Trump returns to the White House Correspondents' Dinner stage as the WHCA honors Secret Service Officer Gonzales for stopping the armed suspect.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump will return to the stage Friday to deliver remarks at a rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Association dinner at the Waldorf Astoria, just months after gunfire abruptly ended the organization’s annual banquet.

"In April, President Trump promised to finish the job and plan another WHCA Dinner, and on Friday, he will deliver on that promise. We look forward to a great and fun evening celebrating 250 years of American greatness and a free press," White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt told Fox News Digital.

Trump attended the original dinner in April during the nation’s 250th-anniversary year. He skipped the event throughout his first term amid repeated clashes with the press. This month's dinner is expected to be a night of unity with a more intimate guest list after the April dinner devolved into chaos.

"This dinner will not only be an opportunity to carry out our program. It will be a statement that violence has no place in American life and a free press will not be intimidated into silence. As you have all demonstrated, courage and community can and should rise above," Weijia Jiang, president of The White House Correspondents’ Association (WHCA), said when the new date was announced.

WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENTS' ASSOCIATION PRESIDENT ANNOUNCES EVENT BEING RESCHEDULED AFTER SHOOTING

The annual dinner has historically been held at the Washington Hilton. Chaos, however, broke out at this year’s banquet when an armed suspect, Cole Allen, stormed the event in what authorities say was an attempt to assassinate the president.

The incident prompted Secret Service agents to rush the president and first lady from the room as guests scrambled for cover, abruptly ending the dinner.

The WHCA will honor U.S. Secret Service Officer Victor Gonzales during Friday's dinner in recognition of his actions in helping stop an armed suspect from entering the ballroom, WHCA announced in a press release Tuesday.

HOW DID SUSPECTED GUNMAN GET WEAPONS INTO CORRESPONDENTS' DINNER? EX-DETECTIVE QUESTIONS SECURITY FAILURES

Gonzales was at a security checkpoint when Cole tried to burst through the Washington Hilton.

"The WHCA is deeply grateful that law enforcement officials prevented the armed suspect from entering the ballroom," said Jiang.

"Officer Gonzales played a significant role when he ran toward danger so that thousands of others could make it home safely. His courage stemmed from a dedication to service, which we are looking forward to honoring this week," she added.

While first lady Melania Trump, Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio will not be in attendance due to prior commitments, many Cabinet officials will be present.

FBI Director Kash Patel, Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick are among those attending.

Mentalist Oz Pearlman will return as entertainment for the guests.

TONY DOKOUPIL CLASHES WITH MARK WARNER AFTER THE SENATOR RIPPED CBS FOR AIRING TRUMP SPEECH ‘AS NEWS’

The April dinner drew dozens of anti-war protesters outside the Washington Hilton as the conflict with Iran dominated headlines, and similar demonstrations could greet attendees Friday as U.S. military operations continue.

The event also comes as Trump has used a string of high-profile speeches to highlight his administration's priorities, including a primetime address last week on election security.

Speaking after the April dinner was cut short, Trump said he hoped the event would be held again and previewed a less combative tone for his eventual remarks. He said he had been prepared to "really rip" the press but decided that delivering such a speech after the shooting would have been inappropriate.

"I think I’m going to be probably very nice. I’ll be very boring the next time. But we’re going to have a great event," Trump said.

item.title: Governor blames activists in Nolan Wells case for ‘stoking division,’ official says FBI probing threats
item.description: The FBI is investigating threats against witnesses and authorities in the Nolan Wells case as Mississippi's governor blames Ben Crump and Al Sharpton, an official said.
item.content:encoded:
MISSISSIPPI – The FBI is investigating threats against the people who were with Nolan Wells in the hours before the Mississippi teen vanished and was found dead, one of the state's top law enforcement officials says.

Wells, 18, disappeared July 4 after joining friends on a boat trip to Horn Island, a barrier island off Mississippi's Gulf Coast. Search crews scoured the area for two days before a National Park Service ranger found his remains on the island's northwestern tip on July 6.

Mississippi Department of Public Safety Commissioner Sean Tindell said Wednesday that the FBI is investigating threats made against public officials, the people who were with Wells before he vanished and their families. Tindell made the comments on Mississippi Public Broadcasting's "The Sit Down" hosted by Magnolia Tribune Founder and CEO Russ Latino.

NOLAN WELLS' HOMETOWN OFFICIALS RALLY BEHIND SHERIFF AFTER 'DERELICT IN THEIR DUTIES' ATTACK

"I'm sure state law enforcement and the FBI is going to be very interested in investigating, so, you know, when you make those types of threats against public officials, against possible witnesses, and against, the, you know, the folks that are involved in this investigation and the family, then you are also, committing a criminal act, and that's going to be taken very, very seriously," Tindell said.

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

Tindell said "false information" shared about Wells' death has incited "rage and emotion" that has led to a higher level of calls coming into his office, some of which are "direct threats." Tindell said he believes Wells' mother, Christine Wonsley, has also received threats.

"I can tell you, the FBI has reached out. They're working in any capacity. They've offered a hand, in this case, to do anything that that's needed from their end. But they're also taking a serious look, at some of these threats that have come across, to public officials, to witnesses to the family, to anybody involved in this case," Tindell said.

NOLAN WELLS INDEPENDENT AUTOPSY DOESN'T POINT TO FOUL PLAY, FORMER MEDICAL EXAMINER SAYS

The warning from one of Mississippi's top public safety officials comes as national attention on the case has reached a fever pitch, with Rep. André Carson, D-Ind., accusing officials in Mississippi of not being transparent.

"Not only are [Nolan Wells' family] suffering the unimaginable grief of losing a child, they're not getting the truth, transparency, or accountability. Mississippi officials retained some of Nolan's body parts, so yesterday's independent autopsy leaves more questions unanswered," Carson said.

LIKE WHAT YOU'RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE

📩 Send me a story idea: Adam.Sabes@fox.com

➡️ X: @asabes10

As the speculation has grown, Mississippi's top elected officials have begun pushing back publicly.

SEARCH CALLED OFF AFTER BODY MATCHING MISSING TEEN NOLAN WELLS FOUND OFF MISSISSIPPI COAST

Republican Mississippi Gov. Tate Reeves told Fox News Digital in a statement that attorney Ben Crump and Al Sharpton are responsible for inciting the threats.

"Ben Crump and Al Sharpton are to blame for the threats. They are stoking division for personal gain. It’s sad to see, but not unexpected. This is how they’ve built their careers," Reeves said. "Jackson County law enforcement, the Mississippi Department of Public Safety, and the State Medical Examiner are conducting a thorough investigation. I’m confident they will follow the facts of the case, wherever that leads them. The state's top prosecutor is also drawing a hard line."

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

Mississippi Attorney General Lynn Fitch told Fox News Digital in a statement that her office will hold accountable anyone who threatens law enforcement working the case.

"I am proud of the professionalism with which our law enforcement, district attorney, and other local leaders are approaching this tragic case. During an open investigation, it is vital to the cause of justice that everyone respect the integrity of the process. Efforts to intimidate, disrupt, or interfere with the work of our law enforcement and prosecutors and threats to their well-being will be taken seriously and we will hold those who make them accountable. A bright young life has been lost and we extend our prayers to his family," Fitch said.

SEND US A TIP

During Wednesday's news conference, Crump revealed that an area of "red discoloration" was found on Wells' head, but said Dr. Roger Mitchell, who performed the autopsy, couldn't come to a conclusion on a cause or manner of death "pending further investigation." An official autopsy into Wells' death hasn't been completed, but the revelation from Crump fueled even more speculation.

Wells' friends returned to the mainland that July afternoon without him, but Jackson County Sheriff John Ledbetter previously said investigators believe Wells chose to stay on the island. It wasn't until later that night that his mother learned he was missing from one of Wells' friends and reported it to authorities.

Fox News Digital has reached out to Crump, Sharpton and the FBI for comment.

item.title: Lock down your ChatGPT account before the next AI attack
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol escaped a locked sandbox, exploited a zero-day vulnerability and compromised Hugging Face servers during a cybersecurity test.
item.content:encoded:
OpenAI has admitted that a combination of its advanced AI models found a way out of a locked-down test environment and compromised another company's systems. That should make every ChatGPT user stop and pay attention. The models were supposed to stay inside a tightly controlled digital sandbox with no open internet access. Instead, they found a weakness, got online and compromised systems belonging to Hugging Face, a major platform for AI models and datasets.

OpenAI described the breach as an "unprecedented cyber incident" involving "state-of-the-art cyber capabilities." The company says the system included GPT-5.6 Sol and an even more powerful model still being tested behind closed doors. Here is what concerns me. OpenAI built the models and controlled the testing environment, yet the systems still found a way around the safeguards designed to contain them.

The AI models were trying to complete a cybersecurity challenge. They apparently had no goal of damaging Hugging Face. However, they kept pushing until they found vulnerabilities and crossed into another company's infrastructure. That should be a wake-up call. You cannot control what happens inside OpenAI's labs, but you can take a few minutes to lock down your ChatGPT account before an AI-powered threat comes looking for your password, personal files or private conversations.

CyberGuy Live: Missed "Sick of Spam?" Get the replay and checklist

Our free CyberGuy Live class, "Sick of Spam?" , has ended, but you can still watch the full replay and download our spam-stopping checklist. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson walks you step by step through simple ways to reduce robocalls, spam texts, junk email and unwanted messages. You’ll also learn how to curb political texts, clean up your inbox and spot messages that could put your personal information at risk.

Get the free replay and checklist now at CyberGuyLive.com.

OPENAI'S SAM ALTMAN WANTS TO NEGOTIATE A 5% STAKE IN COMPANY FOR US IF COMPETITORS AGREE TO KEY PROVISION

OpenAI designed the internal evaluation to measure how well its models could find and exploit difficult security vulnerabilities. The company ran the test without some of the production safety systems that normally block high-risk cyber activity. OpenAI said researchers removed those protections so they could measure the models' maximum capabilities.

The models operated inside a restricted testing environment. Internet access was limited to an internally hosted service that acted as a proxy for software packages. However, the models found a previously unknown vulnerability in that service. They exploited the weakness and moved through OpenAI's research environment until they reached a computer with internet access.

Once online, the models identified Hugging Face as a possible source of answers for the ExploitGym security benchmark. The models then searched for information that could help them complete the test. OpenAI said they combined several attack methods. Those methods included stolen credentials and previously unknown vulnerabilities.

In one case, the models found a path that allowed remote code execution on Hugging Face servers. That gave them the ability to run code on another company's infrastructure. OpenAI said the models remained focused on completing the evaluation. Yet their narrow goal still led them to cross security boundaries and compromise an outside company.

OpenAI says the incident exposed a growing gap between what advanced models can do and the safeguards designed to contain them. "The primary lesson from this incident is that model security and safety must keep pace with rapidly advancing capabilities," OpenAI said in its incident report.

Hugging Face first disclosed the breach on July 16, 2026. The company said an autonomous AI agent system carried out the intrusion from beginning to end. The attack involved thousands of automated actions across short-lived digital environments.

Hugging Face identified unauthorized access to a limited set of internal datasets. The attacker also accessed several credentials used by its services. However, the company found no evidence that anyone altered its public models or user-facing datasets. It also verified that its software supply chain remained clean.

Hugging Face closed the vulnerabilities used for the initial access. It also rebuilt affected systems and rotated exposed credentials. Meanwhile, the company advised Hugging Face customers to rotate their access tokens and review recent activity. That guidance applies to Hugging Face accounts rather than consumer ChatGPT accounts. OpenAI later determined that its models caused the activity during its internal evaluation. The two companies continue to investigate the incident together.

CyberGuy reached out to OpenAI and Hugging Face for comment but did not receive a response before publication.

FBI HELPS TAKE DOWN AI PHISHING RING

OpenAI's disclosure does not identify consumer ChatGPT accounts as part of the incident. The company has also issued no incident-related instruction telling ChatGPT users to reset their passwords. Therefore, this article should not leave you thinking someone breached your personal ChatGPT account.

The larger warning comes from what the models managed to accomplish. They searched for software weaknesses and found an unknown vulnerability. Next, they moved through restricted systems and used stolen credentials to reach an outside target. OpenAI says models such as GPT-5.6 Sol can sustain complex cyber operations over long periods. The company also says the incident shows these abilities can work against real-world systems.

That raises the stakes for any account containing valuable information. Your ChatGPT history may include private conversations and uploaded files. Developers may also have API keys connected to paid OpenAI usage. Good account security cannot stop an AI model from finding a vulnerability inside a major company. However, it can make your account much harder to take over.

AMAZON RECALL TEXT SCAM COMES WITH RED FLAGS

OpenAI now offers several security controls for personal ChatGPT accounts. Availability can vary by account, device and sign-in method. Start with the settings you have today. Then add stronger protections as they become available to your account.

A unique password helps protect your ChatGPT account when another website suffers a breach. OpenAI recommends using a password manager to create and store a strong password. The company also advises changing your password immediately when you believe someone exposed or shared it. To add or update your ChatGPT password:

Changing your ChatGPT password updates the password across your OpenAI account. That includes the API Platform. If you originally registered through Google, Microsoft or Apple, you may not have an OpenAI password to change. Instead, secure the outside account that you use to sign in. Also protect the email address connected to ChatGPT. Anyone who controls your inbox may receive verification messages or password-reset emails.

Multi-factor authentication adds another verification step during sign-in. Even when someone gets your password, they still need access to your second verification method. OpenAI may offer an authenticator app, push notification, text message or passkey. Available choices depend on your account and device. To turn on MFA:

An authenticator app may ask you to scan a QR code. Text-message verification may ask for your phone number. OpenAI will use the most secure enabled option first when you have more than one method available. Keep in mind that enabling MFA does not close sessions that are already active. Change an exposed password first. Then review your active sessions.

A passkey lets you sign in without typing your password. It uses a secure credential stored on your device or a compatible security key. You can protect the passkey with Face ID, Touch ID or your device PIN. Some passkeys can also sync across your devices. To add a passkey:

After setup, ChatGPT may use your passkey as the default sign-in method. The passkey may also work as an additional MFA check. The Passkeys option may not appear on every account. Availability depends on how you created the account and which sign-in method you use. If you store a passkey on only one device, losing that device could create access problems. A synced passkey can offer an easier backup route.

Someone who gained access to your account may remain signed in after you change other security settings. ChatGPT's Active sessions page can show browsers and first-party OpenAI apps connected to your account. Details may include the device, approximate location and sign-in time.

To review your sessions:

To remove one session:

To close every session:

This closes your current session along with the others. OpenAI says the process may take up to 30 minutes. Active sessions does not display every possible connection. It excludes third-party app sessions and connected apps. It also excludes Codex CLI sessions. The feature may also be unavailable when an organization controls the account through single sign-on.

Eligible personal accounts may offer a feature called Advanced Account Security. This setting replaces password-based access with passkeys or compatible security keys. It also disables email sign-in codes and SMS sign-in codes. However, the stronger protection comes with more responsibility. You must keep your secure sign-in methods and recovery keys safe. Before you enroll, you need at least two secure sign-in methods. At least one must work across devices.

To set it up:

ChatGPT will sign you out of every device after setup. You must then sign in with a passkey or security key. Advanced Account Security also turns on login-alert emails and shortens active sessions. In addition, OpenAI says conversations will not be used to train its models while the setting remains enabled. Store your recovery keys away from the devices you use to access ChatGPT. Each recovery key works only once. Losing every sign-in method and your recovery keys could leave you unable to regain access. OpenAI Support cannot restore normal access by resetting your password while Advanced Account Security remains enabled.

Lockdown Mode helps reduce the risk of data leaving ChatGPT during a prompt injection attack. A prompt injection can hide malicious instructions inside a website or uploaded file. Those instructions may try to manipulate how an AI handles sensitive information. Lockdown Mode limits outbound network access that an attacker could use to receive that information. However, it cannot prevent every prompt injection from affecting a response.

To turn it on:

Lockdown Mode restricts live browsing and disables deep research. It also blocks agent mode and file downloads for data analysis. You can still upload a file for ChatGPT to examine. Image generation also remains available. Lockdown Mode may affect connected services and web-based images. It does not change your memory settings or data controls. When you need a blocked feature, you can turn off Lockdown Mode for one conversation. A status message above the message box provides that option.

OpenAI may send a push notification through the ChatGPT app when it detects a login attempt. Approve the notification only when you started the login. Deny access when the request appears unexpectedly. OpenAI may also email a six-digit one-time password. The company says legitimate verification emails may come from these addresses:

noreply@tm.openai.com

otp@tm1.openai.com

Check the sender carefully before using a code. OpenAI advises changing your password immediately when you receive a login alert from an unfamiliar device or location. Never give a verification code to someone who contacts you. Open the official ChatGPT app or type the website address yourself instead of following an unexpected link.

Move quickly when you see conversations you did not start or unfamiliar account activity.

OpenAI deserves credit for disclosing what happened and working with Hugging Face. Still, the company's own report shows that its models crossed a security barrier and reached another company's production systems. The models kept working after they encountered restrictions. That persistence helped them uncover a zero-day vulnerability and find credentials they could use. This should push AI companies and regulators to move faster on containment. Strong monitoring also needs to stay active during the tests designed to measure dangerous capabilities. Consumers cannot build safeguards for frontier AI laboratories. However, you can reduce your exposure by protecting your login and closing sessions you no longer use. When the company building the AI says its own test environment could not contain it, people deserve visible safeguards and fast disclosure when those protections fail.

Would you trust an autonomous AI agent with your banking or personal data after learning that another agent escaped its own security test? Let us know by writing to us at Cyberguy.com

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 lineup adds three choices
item.description: Samsung's three new foldable phones run Android 17 with One UI 9 and Galaxy AI, but each targets a very different user with unique screen sizes.
item.content:encoded:
Samsung is giving those of you who are looking to buy a new phone more choices this week with three new Galaxy Z foldable models built for very different kinds of use.

The Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra focuses on productivity, cameras and a larger workspace. The regular Galaxy Z Fold8 takes a wider approach for reading, gaming and watching content. Meanwhile, the Galaxy Z Flip8 puts more apps and AI tools on its outer screen.

Here is how the three new models compare and what you should know before deciding whether one belongs in your pocket.

CyberGuy Live: Missed "Sick of Spam?" Get the replay and checklist

Our free CyberGuy Live class, "Sick of Spam?" , has ended, but you can still watch the full replay and download our spam-stopping checklist. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson walks you step by step through simple ways to reduce robocalls, spam texts, junk email and unwanted messages. You’ll also learn how to curb political texts, clean up your inbox and spot messages that could put your personal information at risk.

Get the free replay and checklist now at CyberGuyLive.com.

AMAZON LEO SATELLITE INTERNET TAKES ON STARLINK

The Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra is the model for someone who wants a phone that can also serve as a small tablet. It opens to an 8-inch main screen, giving you room to view two apps at once. You could keep your email open beside your calendar or watch a video while looking up related information. The 6.5-inch outer screen also works more like the display on a traditional smartphone, so you do not need to open the phone for every task.

Samsung says the Fold8 Ultra measures about 0.16 inches thick when opened and weighs about 7.6 ounces. That is fairly light for a phone with such a large folding screen. Still, you will notice more bulk than you would with a traditional phone. The Fold8 Ultra also has the most capable camera system in the lineup. Its 200 MP main camera should capture more detail, especially when you crop or enlarge a photo. A 50 MP ultra-wide camera helps with landscapes and group pictures. You also get a separate telephoto camera with 3x optical zoom.

That zoom lens could be useful when you are photographing a child onstage, an animal across the yard or a landmark that you cannot approach. The other two phones lack a dedicated telephoto camera.

A 5,000 mAh battery powers the phone. Samsung supports 45-watt wired charging, although you need to buy a compatible charger separately. The phone also includes extra cooling to help it stay responsive while you use several apps or record high-resolution video.

The Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra starts at $2,099.99 with 256 GB of storage. Samsung also sells 512 GB and 1 TB versions.

The regular Galaxy Z Fold8 takes a different approach. Instead of following the taller design of previous Fold phones, it uses a shorter and wider shape. The 5.5-inch cover screen handles quick tasks while the phone is closed. Open it and you get a 7.6-inch main display with a shape closer to a small tablet.

That wider screen could make articles, e-books and websites more comfortable to read. It should also give movies and games more room without leaving as much unused space around the picture. At about 7.1 ounces, Samsung calls this its lightest Galaxy Z Fold so far. The phone measures about 0.18 inches thick when opened and 0.38 inches when closed. That lower weight could make it easier to hold during a long reading session or video call.

The Fold8 has two 50 MP rear cameras. One handles everyday photos while the other captures a wider view. You can still take detailed pictures and record video in up to 8K resolution. However, there is no dedicated telephoto camera. Digital zoom reaches 10x, but it will not provide the same clarity as the Ultra model's optical zoom. The Fold8 uses the same Snapdragon processor found in the Ultra and carries a 4,800 mAh battery. It also supports 45-watt wired charging with a compatible charger sold separately.

The Galaxy Z Fold8 starts at $1,899.99 with 256 GB of storage. You can also choose 512 GB or 1 TB.

The Galaxy Z Flip8 is the most compact model. It looks like a traditional smartphone when open, then folds in half so it takes up less room in a pocket or purse. The main screen measures 6.9 inches. When you close the phone, a 4.1-inch FlexWindow remains available on the outside.

You can use that smaller screen to check the weather, view your calendar or respond to supported messages. You may also access some apps and AI tools without opening the phone. This could help when you only need one piece of information. Instead of opening your phone and becoming distracted by other alerts, you can check what you need from the outside screen.

The Flip8 weighs about 6.3 ounces and measures about 0.24 inches thick when opened. It is the lightest of Samsung's three new foldables. The Flip8 includes a 50 MP main camera and a 12 MP ultra-wide camera. Its folding design also allows the phone to stand on a table by itself.

That can make hands-free video calls or group photos easier. You can place the phone on a table, adjust the angle and step into the picture without finding someone to hold it. The outer screen can show a preview when you take a selfie with the main camera. Samsung also added tools that help keep video level while you move. The 4,300 mAh battery supports 25-watt wired charging. Samsung says the phone can reach up to 55% in about 30 minutes under its laboratory conditions. The required charger comes separately.

The Galaxy Z Flip8 starts at $1,199.99 with 256 GB of storage. A 512 GB version is also available.

HOW TO SHARE VACATION PHOTOS ON ANY SCREEN

All three phones run Android 17 with Samsung’s One UI 9 software. They also include two Galaxy AI features that serve different purposes.

Now Brief works like a personalized daily summary. It can gather the weather, upcoming appointments and reminders in one place, saving you from opening several apps. You can choose which types of information appear in the summary.

Now Nudge suggests a helpful next step based on what is happening on your screen. For example, if someone mentions a date while you are exchanging messages, the phone may offer to open your calendar. On the Fold models, it can place the calendar beside the conversation so you can check your schedule without leaving the message.

Gemini Intelligence can handle broader requests across supported apps. You might ask it to find a restaurant or help with a reservation. I would still review any important action involving travel, appointments or purchases before approving it.

Samsung has also added an AI Assistant Activity dashboard. It gives you one place to review supported actions the phone completed on your behalf. Privacy Alerts can warn you when an app tries to use certain permissions in the background, while Knox and Knox Vault help protect sensitive information.

Samsung uses a new Flex Titanium structure beneath the folding displays. The company says this helps support the screens and reduce the visibility of the crease. The two Fold screens can reach up to 3,000 nits of brightness. Samsung also uses a low-reflection finish to make the displays easier to see outdoors. All three phones carry an IP48 water resistance rating.

Samsung has updated Smart Switch for people moving from an iPhone. You can scan a QR code and transfer supported information wirelessly without installing another app on the iPhone. Samsung says the transfer can include passwords, passkeys and call history. Some accessibility settings and eSIM information may also move, depending on your device and carrier.

Quick Share also gains compatibility with AirDrop. That means supported Galaxy and Apple devices can exchange files in both directions. Moving years of photos and account information can make switching phones feel like a chore. These updates could make the move to Galaxy much easier.

Preorders are open through Samsung, major U.S. carriers and participating retailers. General availability begins Aug. 7, 2026. The Fold8 Ultra comes in Graphite, Cream and Violet Shadow. Green Shadow is an online-exclusive color.

The Fold8 comes in Graphite, Cream and Lavender. Samsung offers Pistachio as an online-exclusive choice. The Flip8 comes in Graphite, Cream and Pink. Mint is the online-exclusive option.

Samsung advertises up to $1,200 in savings with an eligible trade-in during the preorder period. Buyers who choose no trade-in may receive up to $200 in Samsung credit for eligible add-ons. Offers depend on the phone and trade-in condition. Each model also includes a six-month Google AI Pro trial with 5 TB of cloud storage. The service costs $19.99 per month after the trial unless you cancel.

Samsung has made the differences between its foldables much easier to understand. The Fold8 Ultra focuses on a large workspace and stronger cameras. The regular Fold8 favors reading and entertainment, while the Flip8 gives you a phone that becomes smaller when you close it. I like that Samsung now offers two versions of the Fold for different types of use. The new AI activity dashboard also gives you more visibility when the phone takes action on your behalf. The biggest question is whether one of these designs would truly make your phone easier to use every day. That answer will depend on how much you value a larger screen, a more compact shape or Samsung's newest AI tools.

Do you think a larger folding screen would make your phone easier to use? Let us know by writing to us at Cyberguy.com

Sign up for my FREE CyberGuy Report
